root_state: Decision: ship a customer-facing change by Friday or wait for a broader refactor. Facts: customer impact is high, rollback is easy, refactor risk is medium, deadline is Friday. Belief: asking for a narrower pilot may look weak. Preference: choose a low-regret reversible move.

node.state: Decision: ship a customer-facing change by Friday or wait for a broader refactor. Facts: customer impact is high, rollback is easy, refactor risk is medium, deadline is Friday. Belief: asking for a narrower pilot may look weak. Preference: choose a low-regret reversible move.

assumptions_used: speed matters more than reversibility
beliefs_tested: the boldest visible action is the best action
node_notes: This branch optimizes momentum but has weak downside control.
child.rationale: Fast, but it assumes urgency matters more than reversibility and risk control.
child.state: State S1: full launch happens before the refactor is ready

parent.state: Decision: ship a customer-facing change by Friday or wait for a broader refactor. Facts: customer impact is high, rollback is easy, refactor risk is medium, deadline is Friday. Belief: asking for a narrower pilot may look weak. Preference: choose a low-regret reversible move.

assumptions_used: a smaller pilot can still create visible customer value
beliefs_tested: asking for a narrower pilot looks weak
node_notes: This branch converts the psychological belief into a testable communication problem.
child.rationale: Balances customer urgency with a reversible path and gives the team information.
child.state: State S2: a smaller launch creates evidence while preserving rollback

parent.state: Decision: ship a customer-facing change by Friday or wait for a broader refactor. Facts: customer impact is high, rollback is easy, refactor risk is medium, deadline is Friday. Belief: asking for a narrower pilot may look weak. Preference: choose a low-regret reversible move.

assumptions_used: waiting will materially improve the release quality
beliefs_tested: perfecting the implementation beats learning from a small release
node_notes: This branch is safer technically but weak on time-to-learn.
child.rationale: Reduces implementation risk but under-serves the high customer impact before Friday.
child.state: State S3: release waits for the broader refactor

beliefs_tested: a staged plan looks weak to stakeholders

child.rationale: Creates customer value, preserves reversibility, and tests whether the weak-looking pilot belief is real.
child.state: State S2b: a limited segment gets the change with rollback ready

tree_synthesis.robust_takeaway: run_reversible_pilot is the selected path; delay_for_refactor is the closest alternative. Use the runner-up if the selected path's assumptions fail.
selected_path_assumptions: a smaller pilot can still create visible customer value
a limited segment is enough to learn
reviewing pilot evidence is acceptable to stakeholders
selected_path_beliefs_tested: asking for a narrower pilot looks weak
a staged plan looks weak to stakeholders
visible progress plus evidence is stronger than full launch theater
